commit.message:
Move Elixir observability dashboard to Phoenix (#29)

#### Context

Replace the custom Elixir observability TCP server with Phoenix while
keeping the shipped escript and existing operational API calls working
the same way they did before.

#### TL;DR

*Move the Elixir observability dashboard and API onto Phoenix without
breaking the escript or curl workflows.*

#### Summary

- replace the custom HTTP server with a Phoenix endpoint, router,
controller, and shared presenter
- add a LiveView operations dashboard with PubSub-driven updates and
embed its CSS and JS assets in code
- preserve runtime compatibility by accepting form-style POSTs and
extending the endpoint coverage around those paths

#### Alternatives

- keep extending the hand-rolled TCP server, but that keeps custom
parsing and routing complexity in the app
- serve dashboard assets from priv directories, but the shipped escript
cannot rely on those files existing on disk

#### Test Plan

- [x] `make -C elixir all`
- [x] `mix -C elixir test test/symphony_elixir/extensions_test.exs`
- [x] `./bin/symphony
--i-understand-that-this-will-be-running-without-the-usual-guardrails
--port 40123`
- [x] `curl -si http://127.0.0.1:40123/dashboard.css`
- [x] `curl -si http://127.0.0.1:40123/vendor/phoenix/phoenix.js`
- [x] `curl -si -X POST -d '' http://127.0.0.1:40123/api/v1/refresh`
- [x] `curl -si -X POST -d '' http://127.0.0.1:40123/api/v1/state`

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: symphony
repository.description: Symphony turns project work into isolated, autonomous implementation runs, allowing teams to manage work instead of supervising coding agents.

[fix](trx-server): hard-gate CW decode to CW/CWR modes

Enforce CW decode processing only when mode is CW/CWR and decoder
is enabled, even within the receive loop, and remove CW path RMS
zero-gating to preserve weak tone decoding.

commit.message:
[fix](trx-cw): stabilize auto tone scan window

Keep manual CW tone setting range at 100-10000 Hz, but limit auto-tone
scanning to 300-1200 Hz to avoid locking onto high-frequency noise.
Retain Nyquist-safe upper clamping.

commit.message:
[fix](trx-server): gate near-silent audio before decode

Apply an RMS gate to near-silent decoder input frames and zero them
before APRS/CW/FT8/WSPR processing to avoid false decodes from
unsquelched/no-signal noise.

commit.message:
(docs) Migrate website from DocFX to MkDocs Material

Replace the DocFX documentation build with MkDocs Material and migrate
docs tooling to uv with locked dependencies.

Keep Perlang example rendering by translating both code-perlang tags and
fenced perlang blocks through a MkDocs hook, while preserving custom
Perlang syntax highlighting and asciinema embeds.

Remove obsolete DocFX files/templates and update GitHub/GitLab CI to
validate website builds with the new pipeline.

Closes https://gitlab.perlang.org/perlang/perlang/-/issues/515

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Centralize capability auth completion normalization

Move callback/code parsing and validation into capability manager, add explicit auth completion error taxonomy, and pass normalized auth payloads to providers/bridge.

Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

ledger: unify runtime dispatch and harden hook/store reliability

Unify lifecycle extraction across Codex/OpenCode payload shapes and improve
Ledger durability/observability in hooks and NDJSON storage.

- Add runtime dispatcher + adapters and shared input normalization.
- Update claim-gate/lifecycle contract:
  - blank owner => non-claim path
  - claim-gate no-ops `PostToolUse` in tests
  - lifecycle path logs processing errors to stderr
- Improve nonblocking emit diagnostics (store-init/timeout/append-error).
- Make store factory singleton init thread-safe.
- Harden NDJSON store:
  - empty state file treated as invalid (fallback scan)
  - malformed rows tolerated
  - atomic state writes + stale-state cleanup on partial failure
  - process-safe non-POSIX lock fallback
- Expand tests for contract boundaries, codex-first ambiguous dispatch,
  emit diagnostics, state recovery, and lock behavior.
- Add/update docs:
  - runtime-agnostic workflow contract (`docs/ledger-workflow.md`)
  - Claude hooks doc pointer + index updates
  - OpenCode plugin reference and Codex hook-method notes in impl plan
- Include repo guidance and tooling updates (`AGENTS.md`, `conftest.py`,
  `scripts/codex-notify-log.py`).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Refactor statement codegen context and annotation setup

- move statement codegen away from threaded context parameters and simplify
  statement-list emission helpers
- remove unused `cg_state.current_function` state and collapse its remaining
  call-site scaffolding
- remove delayed `setup_annotations` state and emit `SETUP_ANNOTATIONS`
  eagerly for non-function statement lists based on scope analysis
- extract shared non-function body preamble (`annotations` + `__doc__`) and
  reuse it in module/class emission paths
- inline small single-use helpers (`emit_module_begin`/`emit_module_end` and
  function-closure setup in `emit_def`) to reduce indirection

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: implement Phase 1 - Foundation & Error Collection

## Requirements Implemented

- INT-01: OpenClaw Plugin skeleton with manifest
- INT-02: Reuse .learnings/ directory structure
- INT-03: CLI command entry point (openclaw forcefield)
- ERR-01: Tool call failure detection (tool_result_persist hook)
- ERR-02: User correction detection (agent_end hook)
- ERR-03: Error context collection
- ERR-04: Error writing to .learnings/ERRORS.md
- ERR-05: CLI manual error addition

## Files Added

- src/index.ts - Plugin entry point
- src/cli.ts - CLI commands
- src/hooks/toolResultPersist.ts - Tool failure detection
- src/hooks/agentEnd.ts - User correction detection
- src/storage/errorWriter.ts - Error persistence
- src/storage/errorReader.ts - Error retrieval
- src/commands/errorAdd.ts - Manual error addition
- src/commands/errorList.ts - List errors
- src/types/error.ts - Error types
- src/types/rule.ts - Rule types
- tests/unit/*.test.ts - Unit tests
- tests/e2e/cli.flow.test.ts - E2E tests

## Test Results

- 5 tests passing
- Type check passing

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(agent): add dispatch watch transcript fixtures

Add transcript-style regression fixtures for dispatch watch boundary patterns and replay them in AC-13/AC-14 tests to lock formatting semantics across adapter chunking variants.

Verification:
- npx vitest run tests/cli-agent-commands.test.ts
- npm run test:shard3
- kspec validate (fails due pre-existing meta/reference/alignment issues unrelated to this change)

Task: @01KJVM89E3XNHP6YA3X1NTRJ7G
Spec: @cli-agent-commands
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(agent): fail fast on dispatch watch subscribe rejection

Handle failed subscribe acknowledgements in kspec agent dispatch watch by surfacing actionable guidance and exiting with runtime error code instead of silently waiting for output.

Adds AC-18 coverage for subscribe-handshake failure semantics and validates the new behavior in cli-agent-commands tests.

Task: @01KJVD7HDS5HVJ4JAE6M7SPV9R
Spec: @cli-agent-commands
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: codex向けsession-noteスキル管理を追加

- codex/skills/session-note/SKILL.md を追加
- install.sh に Codex スキルのリンク処理を追加
- README に Codex 管理対象を追記

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test: add reusable startup wait helper for daemon tests

Introduce a shared bounded polling helper for startup/readiness checks and migrate daemon/process test waits away from ad-hoc fixed-delay assertions.

Task: @01KJTP69W21Z3GQE8J2HAXC4WV

Spec: @daemon-sensitive-cli-test-determinism

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

test: share HOME isolation helper for daemon CLI tests

Add a shared helper to isolate HOME/.config/kspec for daemon-sensitive CLI tests and migrate existing ad-hoc env setup to it.

Task: @01KJTP68SV9VZ37KXF6TCEEKWB

Spec: @daemon-sensitive-cli-test-determinism

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(test): isolate AC-10 daemon dispatch tests

Disable daemon auto-start in the AC-10 fixture and force per-test HOME/USERPROFILE isolation so subprocess CLI runs cannot attach to external daemons from parallel shards.

commit.message:
feat(session-log): add --text replay for session log show

Add a --text flag to session log show to replay assistant output from session.update content blocks. The replay path resolves blob pointers before extraction and supports both content arrays and chunk-style content objects.

Also adds integration coverage for AC @session-log-show ac-11 and verifies --json --text --events coexistence.

Task: @01KJS7PDW5GQQX9WQ2C7C1JNRX
Spec: @session-log-show
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>
